steps.title: Find the perpendicular slope
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation of line $s$ in slope-intercept form.
$$10x+2y=0$$
$$2y=-10x$$
$$y=-5x$$
Line $s$ has slope $-5$, so line $t$ has slope $\frac{1}{5}$.

steps.title: Use the point on line t
steps.content:
Line $t$ passes through $(7,0)$, so its equation is
$$y=\frac{1}{5}(x-7)$$
Substitute the point $(k,\frac{6}{5})$.
$$\frac{6}{5}=\frac{1}{5}(k-7)$$
$$6=k-7$$
$$k=13$$
The answer is 13.
